---
package: "openpipeline"
version: "v4.2.0"
title: "from_10xh5_to_h5mu"
namespace: "Convert"
description: "Converts a 10x h5 into an h5mu file"
type: "module"
toc: false
latest: "true"
subtitle: "openpipeline v4.2.0"
---

::: {.column-margin}

### Info

ID: `from_10xh5_to_h5mu`  
Namespace: `convert`

### Links

[{{< fa brands github >}} View source code](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline/tree/v4.2.0/src/convert/from_10xh5_to_h5mu)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Browse all versions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Get run instructions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/convert/from_10xh5_to_h5mu)

:::



## Example commands

You can run the pipeline using `nextflow run`.

### View help

You can use `--help` as a parameter to get an overview of the possible parameters.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -main-script target/nextflow/convert/from_10xh5_to_h5mu/main.nf \
  --help
```

### Run command

<details>
<summary>Example of `params.yaml`</summary>

Generate a `params.yaml` for this component on [Viash Hub](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/convert/from_10xh5_to_h5mu).

</details>

Run locally with:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/convert/from_10xh5_to_h5mu/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

:::{.callout-note}
Replace `-profile docker` with `-profile podman` or `-profile singularity` depending on the desired backend.
:::

Running on Seqera or through Viash Hub is explained in the [guides](/guides/index.qmd).

## Argument groups

### Inputs

|Name                      |Description                                             |Attributes                                              |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--input`                 |A 10x h5 file as generated by Cell Ranger.              |`file`, required, example: `"raw_feature_bc_matrix.h5"` |
|`--input_metrics_summary` |A metrics summary csv file as generated by Cell Ranger. |`file`, example: `"metrics_cellranger.csv"`             |


### Outputs

|Name                   |Description                                                                                                    |Attributes                                |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--output`             |Output h5mu file.                                                                                              |`file`, example: `"output.h5mu"`          |
|`--uns_metrics`        |Name of the .uns slot under which to QC metrics (if any).                                                      |`string`, default: `"metrics_cellranger"` |
|`--output_compression` |Compression format to use for the output AnnData and/or Mudata H5 files. By default no compression is applied. |`string`, example: `"gzip"`               |


### Arguments

|Name           |Description                                                              |Attributes                 |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--min_genes`  |Minimum number of counts required for a cell to pass filtering.          |`integer`, example: `100`  |
|`--min_counts` |Minimum number of genes expressed required for a cell to pass filtering. |`integer`, example: `1000` |

## Authors


  * Robrecht Cannoodt [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/rcannood) [{{< fa brands orcid >}}](https://orcid.org/0000-0003-3641-729X) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/robrechtcannoodt) (maintainer)
